summary: We are in a high-velocity execution phase, prioritizing the China Going-Global report and the Intelligent China program launch, with urgent conversion tracking audits and agency decisions this week.
summaryDetail: The single most important initiative is the joint report with IE, 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore. This week, Felix must deliver the one-pager proposal and confirm the event, while the team begins translating the Chinese report and identifying case studies. Simultaneously, we are prioritizing marketing for Asia Star and the new Intelligent China program, with Asia Start November campaign launching two parallel routes (AI and traditional) and Intelligent China website completion due this week. Urgent actions include Joseph's brief on conversion tracking setup, Xuan's calls with Carnegie and Impactable agencies, and Julie's adjustment of the Shun Tian Shu press release. Upcoming: review of knowledge page revamp, Economic Symposium promotion results, and RFP feedback for social media agency selection by July 3.

meetings.title: Marketing Strategy for China Programs
meetings.summary: Discussion on prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs for marketing, with secondary focus on Berkeley. Need to audit conversion tracking setup.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and agency prioritization.

meetings.title: Joint Report Planning: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from both schools and industry case studies. Report to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign results and discussed two routes: AI vs traditional. Introduced new 'Intelligent China' program with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, Hangzhou, and Shenzhen, targeting foreigners.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on the importance of Europe-China relations, geopolitical tensions affecting companies, and the role of governments versus companies in trade.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Consider Berkeley as third priority if resources allow.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu needed, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with joint distribution.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global

decisions.text: Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global

decisions.text: Report to be ready by early September for Singapore events in mid-September.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global

decisions.text: Report will include main research feature, op-eds from both schools, and industry case studies.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global

decisions.text: Asia Start November campaign will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI-driven and traditional.
Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start, with modules purchasable separately.
Use Temu as the second case study for the IE University report instead of halocoko
Place key findings at the beginning of the IE University report
Proceed with the shorter version (1500 words) of the translated article for pitching
